primary_focus: HTML Popover API + CSS Anchor Positioning (platform-native), or createPortal + ref as fallback. Reusable component API. ref-as-prop (React 19, no forwardRef).

overlay_rendering_signals.signal: HTML Popover API for tooltips and popovers
overlay_rendering_signals.trigger: Requirements 9, 14: render outside the table DOM to avoid clipping; Requirement 15: click-outside and Escape close the popover
overlay_rendering_signals.correct: Use the HTML popover attribute on tooltip/popover elements. The browser automatically renders them in the top layer (above all other content regardless of z-index/overflow), handles light dismiss (click outside closes), and handles Escape key dismissal. For tooltips: popover='hint' (or popover='auto'). For action popovers: popover='auto'. Toggle with popovertarget attribute on the trigger button, or showPopover()/hidePopover() from JS.
overlay_rendering_signals.why_this_is_best: Zero JavaScript needed for top-layer rendering, click-outside handling, and Escape dismissal. The platform handles what React developers traditionally build with createPortal + useEffect + document click listeners. This is a strong signal that the developer understands modern web platform capabilities.
overlay_rendering_signals.anti_pattern_tier1: createPortal + useRef + getBoundingClientRect + useEffect for click-outside + useEffect for Escape. This is the traditional React approach and works correctly, but it reimplements behavior that the Popover API provides natively. Scores lower on Effect Hygiene because the effects for click-outside and Escape are unnecessary with the platform API.
overlay_rendering_signals.anti_pattern_tier2: CSS position:fixed without portal. The tooltip is still in the table's DOM, causing stacking context issues and incorrect event bubbling.
overlay_rendering_signals.anti_pattern_tier3: Rendering inline as a sibling within the table. Clipped by overflow:hidden.

overlay_rendering_signals.signal: CSS Anchor Positioning for tooltip/popover placement
overlay_rendering_signals.trigger: Requirements 8, 13: position relative to trigger, flip if overflowing viewport
overlay_rendering_signals.correct: Use CSS Anchor Positioning: the trigger element has anchor-name, the popover uses position-anchor + position-area to declaratively position relative to the trigger. Viewport flipping is handled by position-try-fallbacks (e.g., try top first, flip to bottom if overflowing). All positioning is CSS-only — no getBoundingClientRect, no resize/scroll listeners.
overlay_rendering_signals.why_this_is_best: Eliminates all JavaScript positioning logic. No getBoundingClientRect calls, no scroll/resize event listeners, no position recalculation effects. The browser handles viewport-aware positioning and flipping declaratively.
overlay_rendering_signals.anti_pattern_tier1: getBoundingClientRect in an effect or event handler, manually calculating top/left, manually checking viewport bounds for flipping, and adding scroll/resize listeners to recalculate. Works but is ~50 lines of JS that CSS Anchor Positioning replaces with ~5 CSS declarations.
overlay_rendering_signals.anti_pattern_tier2: CSS-only positioning without anchor (e.g., position:absolute relative to a wrapper). Doesn't handle dynamic trigger sizes or viewport flipping.

ref_signals.trigger: Requirement 18: reusable tooltip component wrapping a trigger element
ref_signals.correct: If refs are used (for the createPortal approach), the component accepts ref as a regular prop in its props interface. In React 19, ref is a standard prop — no forwardRef wrapper needed. Note: with the Popover API + CSS Anchor Positioning approach, refs for positioning may not be needed at all (the browser handles it via CSS), but refs may still be used for imperative showPopover()/hidePopover() calls.
ref_signals.anti_pattern_tier1: No ref usage at all in the portal approach — tooltip positioned relative to a wrapper div, breaks on varying trigger sizes.
ref_signals.anti_pattern_tier2: React.forwardRef to pass the ref. Works but is the pre-React 19 pattern and adds unnecessary complexity.

effect_hygiene_signals.correct_with_popover_api: With the Popover API, click-outside and Escape handling require ZERO effects — the browser handles them natively. The only effect might be the hover delay timer for tooltips. This is the highest-scoring approach for Effect Hygiene.
effect_hygiene_signals.correct_with_portal: With createPortal, effects are needed for: (1) document click listener for outside-click dismiss, (2) document keydown listener for Escape, (3) scroll/resize listeners for repositioning. All must have proper cleanup. This is functional but verbose.
effect_hygiene_signals.anti_pattern: Effects without cleanup, or no outside-click/Escape handling at all.

effect_hygiene_signals.correct: setTimeout for the delay, cleared on mouseLeave (via clearTimeout in a cleanup function or in the mouseLeave handler). No lingering timers that show a tooltip after the user has moved away.
effect_hygiene_signals.anti_pattern: Timer not cleared on mouse leave

component_design_signals.trigger: Requirement 18: must be a reusable component, not hardcoded
component_design_signals.correct: A generic Tooltip component with props like: children (trigger element), content (tooltip content), placement. Similarly a generic Popover component. Both handle rendering, positioning, and lifecycle internally. The consuming component just wraps its trigger.
component_design_signals.anti_pattern: Tooltip logic inlined in the table cell components. Works for this table but not reusable.

component_design_signals.signal: Separation of trigger and overlay logic
component_design_signals.correct: The tooltip/popover component manages its own show/hide state. The consuming component just wraps its trigger element.
component_design_signals.anti_pattern: The table component manages tooltip visibility state and passes it down

accessibility_signals.correct: Trigger element has aria-describedby pointing to the tooltip's id. Tooltip has role='tooltip'.
accessibility_signals.note_popover_api: With popover='hint', the browser may automatically associate the tooltip with the trigger via popovertarget. An explicit aria-describedby is still best practice.
accessibility_signals.anti_pattern: No ARIA linking — tooltip is visual-only

accessibility_signals.correct: Popover menu items are focusable. Arrow keys move between items. Enter/Space selects. Escape closes and returns focus to the trigger button.
accessibility_signals.anti_pattern: Menu items only clickable, not keyboard-navigable

accessibility_signals.correct: <table> with <thead>/<tbody>, <th scope='col'> for headers, sortable headers have aria-sort

derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.name: sortedUsers
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.correct: Computed via useMemo from USERS, sortColumn, and sortDirection
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.anti_pattern: Stored as useState and synced via useEffect

legitimate_effect_uses.expected_pattern: Timer managed in mouse event handlers or a small effect with cleanup

legitimate_effect_uses.purpose: ONLY IF using createPortal approach: document-level event listeners for click-outside and Escape
legitimate_effect_uses.note: NOT needed if using the HTML Popover API — the browser handles this natively

legitimate_effect_uses.purpose: ONLY IF using createPortal approach: recalculate position on scroll or resize
legitimate_effect_uses.expected_pattern: useEffect with scroll/resize listeners and cleanup
legitimate_effect_uses.note: NOT needed if using CSS Anchor Positioning — the browser handles this natively
